annotations.label: Total order

anchor.snippet: a total order or linear order is a partial order in which any two elements are comparable

annotations.note: `LinearOrder` extends `PartialOrder` with the `le_total` field, exactly a partial order in which any two elements are comparable.

annotations.label: Reflexivity from strong connectedness

anchor.snippet: Reflexivity (1.) already follows from strong connectedness

annotations.note: Mathlib takes reflexivity as a `Preorder` axiom (`le_refl`) rather than deriving it from totality, so this derivation is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Totally ordered set

anchor.snippet: A set equipped with a total order is a totally ordered set

annotations.note: A type carrying a `LinearOrder` instance is precisely a totally ordered set.

annotations.label: Linear extension

anchor.snippet: is called a linear extension of that partial order

annotations.note: The Szpilrajn theorem `extend_partialOrder` (and `LinearExtension`) gives a linear order extending any given partial order.

annotations.label: Strict total order associated

anchor.snippet: called the strict total order associated with

annotations.note: For any `LinearOrder α`, the `<` relation has an `IsStrictTotalOrder α (· < ·)` instance, the strict order associated with `≤`.

annotations.label: Reflexive closure of strict total order

anchor.snippet: the reflexive closure of a strict total order

annotations.note: `le_iff_lt_or_eq` states `a ≤ b ↔ a < b ∨ a = b`, i.e. `≤` is the reflexive closure of `<` (and `linearOrderOfSTO` builds the order from a strict total order).

annotations.label: Strict total order

anchor.snippet: in which any two distinct elements are comparable

annotations.note: `IsStrictTotalOrder` is a trichotomous strict order, matching a relation in which any two distinct elements are comparable.

annotations.label: Asymmetry from transitivity and irreflexivity

anchor.snippet: Asymmetry follows from transitivity and irreflexivity

annotations.note: The instance `asymm_of_isTrans_of_irrefl [IsTrans α r] [Std.Irrefl r] : Std.Asymm r` derives asymmetry from transitivity and irreflexivity.

annotations.label: Subset of totally ordered set

anchor.snippet: Any subset of a totally ordered set X is totally ordered for the restriction of the order on X

annotations.note: `Subtype.instLinearOrder` gives a `LinearOrder` on any subtype `{x // p x}` of a linear order.

annotations.label: Order on empty set

anchor.snippet: The unique order on the empty set

annotations.note: `instance : LinearOrder Empty := .ofSubsingleton` provides the (necessarily unique) linear order on the empty type.

annotations.label: Ordinal numbers

anchor.snippet: Any set of ordinal numbers

annotations.note: `Ordinal` carries a `LinearOrder` instance (indeed a well order), so any set of ordinals is totally ordered.

annotations.label: Induced order via injective function

anchor.snippet: f induces a total ordering on X by setting

annotations.note: `LinearOrder.lift` (and `Function.Injective.linearOrder`) transfers a linear order along an injective map `f : α → β`.

annotations.label: Lexicographical order on a product

anchor.snippet: The lexicographical order on the Cartesian product of a family of totally ordered sets

annotations.note: `Pi.Lex.linearOrder` is the lexicographic linear order on a family `∀ i, β i` of linear orders indexed by a well-founded order.

annotations.label: Real numbers totally ordered

anchor.snippet: The set of real numbers ordered by the usual

annotations.note: `Real.linearOrder` equips ℝ with its usual linear order.

annotations.label: Natural numbers as initial total order

anchor.snippet: The natural numbers form an initial non-empty totally ordered set with no upper bound

annotations.note: ℕ has a `LinearOrder` and `NoMaxOrder` instance, but its characterization as the initial unbounded total order is not formalized.

annotations.label: Integers as initial total order

anchor.snippet: The integers form an initial non-empty totally ordered set with neither an upper nor a lower bound

annotations.note: ℤ has a `LinearOrder` (with no max/min order), but the categorical 'initial' characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rational numbers as initial total order

anchor.snippet: The rational numbers form an initial totally ordered set which is dense in the real numbers

annotations.note: ℚ has a `LinearOrder` and is densely ordered/dense in ℝ, but the 'initial dense total order' characterization itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real numbers as initial unbounded total order

anchor.snippet: The real numbers form an initial unbounded totally ordered set that is connected in the order topology

annotations.note: ℝ's linear order and order-topology connectedness are formalized, but the initiality characterization is not.

annotations.label: Ordered fields

anchor.snippet: Ordered fields are totally ordered by definition

annotations.note: Ordered fields are spelled `[Field K] [LinearOrder K] [IsStrictOrderedRing K]`, so the total order is part of the structure by definition.

annotations.label: Dedekind-complete ordered field

anchor.snippet: Any Dedekind-complete ordered field is isomorphic to the real numbers

annotations.note: `inducedOrderRingIso` gives a unique order-ring isomorphism between any two conditionally complete (Dedekind-complete) linear ordered fields, in particular to ℝ.

annotations.label: Alphabet dictionary order

anchor.snippet: The letters of the alphabet ordered by the standard dictionary order

annotations.note: Finite linear orders (and `List.Lex` dictionary orders) are formalized, but there is no specific 'alphabet' linear order.

annotations.label: Chain

anchor.snippet: it is generally used for referring to a subset of a partially ordered set that is totally ordered

annotations.note: `IsChain` is a set on which the relation is total, i.e. a totally ordered subset.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma

anchor.snippet: if every chain in a partially ordered set X has an upper bound in X

annotations.note: `zorn_le` states that if every chain is bounded above then there is a maximal element.

annotations.label: Ascending and descending chain

anchor.snippet: is called an ascending chain or a descending chain

annotations.note: Mathlib models ascending/descending chains as monotone sequences `ℕ →o α` rather than via a bundled 'chain' definition.

annotations.label: Descending chain condition

anchor.snippet: has the descending chain condition if every descending chain eventually stabilizes

annotations.note: The DCC is `WellFoundedLT` (order-dual of `WellFoundedGT`), and `monotone_chain_condition` proves chains eventually stabilize.

annotations.label: Noetherian ring

anchor.snippet: a Noetherian ring is a ring whose ideals satisfy the ascending chain condition

annotations.note: `IsNoetherianRing` is defined via `IsNoetherian R R` (well-founded `>` / ACC on ideals), with `isNoetherianRing_iff_ideal_fg` an equivalent form.

annotations.label: Length of a chain

anchor.snippet: the length of a chain is the number of inequalities

annotations.note: `RelSeries.length` is the number of relations (inequalities) in a chain/series.

annotations.label: Total order as a lattice

anchor.snippet: One may define a totally ordered set as a particular kind of lattice

annotations.note: `LinearOrder.toLattice` realizes any linear order as a lattice with `min`/`max` as meet/join.

annotations.label: Totally ordered set is distributive lattice

anchor.snippet: Hence a totally ordered set is a distributive lattice

annotations.note: An instance `[LinearOrder α] : DistribLattice α` in Order/Lattice.lean makes every linear order a distributive lattice.

annotations.label: Finite total order is a well order

anchor.snippet: every finite total order is in fact a well order

annotations.note: `Fintype.to_wellFoundedLT` gives `WellFoundedLT` for any finite preorder, hence finite linear orders are well orders.

annotations.label: Finite total order isomorphic to initial segment

anchor.snippet: every finite total order is order isomorphic to an initial segment of the natural numbers

annotations.note: `monoEquivOfFin` is an order isomorphism between a finite linear order and `Fin (card α)`, the initial segment of ℕ.

annotations.label: Total order induces bijection

anchor.snippet: a total order on a set with k elements induces a bijection with the first k natural numbers

annotations.note: `monoEquivOfFin α h : α ≃o Fin k` is exactly the order-preserving bijection with the first `k` naturals.

annotations.label: Tosets as full subcategory

anchor.snippet: Totally ordered sets form a full subcategory of the category of partially ordered sets

annotations.note: The category `LinOrd` exists alongside `PartOrd`, but its identification as a full subcategory of `PartOrd` is not established.

annotations.label: Order-respecting bijection is isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bijective map between two totally ordered sets that respects the two orders is an isomorphism in this category

annotations.note: `StrictMono.orderIsoOfSurjective` upgrades a surjective order-respecting (strictly monotone) map to an `OrderIso`.

annotations.label: Open intervals

anchor.snippet: For any totally ordered set X we can define the open intervals

annotations.note: `Set.Ioo a b = {x | a < x ∧ x < b}` defines the open intervals (with `Ioi`/`Iio` for unbounded ones).

annotations.label: Order topology

anchor.snippet: to define a topology on any ordered set, the order topology

annotations.note: `OrderTopology` (via `Preorder.topology`) is the topology generated by the open intervals/rays.

annotations.label: Order topology is hereditarily normal

anchor.snippet: The order topology induced by a total order may be shown to be hereditarily normal

annotations.note: `OrderTopology.t5Space`/`OrderTopology.completelyNormalSpace` show the order topology is T5 (completely/hereditarily normal).

annotations.label: Complete totally ordered set

anchor.snippet: A totally ordered set is said to be complete if every nonempty subset that has an upper bound

annotations.note: `ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder` is exactly a linear order where every nonempty bounded-above set has a least upper bound.

annotations.label: Connected order topology implies complete

anchor.snippet: If the order topology on X is connected, X is complete

annotations.note: Related results (preconnected order topology implies densely ordered; conditional completeness gives connectedness) exist, but the exact 'connected ⇒ complete' implication is not stated.

annotations.label: Connectedness iff complete with no gap

anchor.snippet: X is connected under the order topology if and only if it is complete and there is no gap in X

annotations.note: Mathlib relates order-connectedness to conditional completeness and density (no gaps) but does not state this exact biconditional.

annotations.label: Completeness iff closed bounded sets compact

anchor.snippet: X is complete if and only if every bounded set that is closed in the order topology is compact

annotations.note: One direction (closed intervals in a conditionally complete order are compact) is `isCompact_Icc`, but the full biconditional characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complete lattice total order is compact

anchor.snippet: which is a complete lattice is compact

annotations.note: `compactSpace_of_completeLinearOrder` shows a complete linear order is a compact space in the order topology.

annotations.label: Sum of two orders

anchor.snippet: which is called the sum of the two orders

annotations.note: `Sum.Lex.linearOrder` is the linear order on `α ⊕ₗ β` placing all of `α` before `β`, the ordinal sum of two orders.

annotations.label: Sum over an index set

anchor.snippet: then the natural total order on

annotations.note: `Sigma.Lex` is the lexicographic order on the indexed sum `Σ i, α i`, the natural total order summing a family of orders over an index set.

annotations.label: Lexicographical order on product

anchor.section: Orders on the Cartesian product of totally ordered sets
anchor.snippet: This is a total order

annotations.note: `Prod.Lex.instLinearOrder` gives the lexicographic linear (total) order on `α ×ₗ β`.

annotations.label: Product order

anchor.section: Orders on the Cartesian product of totally ordered sets
anchor.snippet: This is a partial order

annotations.note: `Prod.instPartialOrder` is the componentwise (product) partial order on `α × β`.

annotations.label: Reflexive closure of direct product

anchor.section: Orders on the Cartesian product of totally ordered sets
anchor.snippet: This is also a partial order

annotations.note: The reflexive closure of the componentwise strict product order (`a<c ∧ b<d`, plus equality) is not provided as a standard order instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real function defines strict weak order

anchor.section: Orders on the Cartesian product of totally ordered sets
anchor.snippet: defines a strict weak order and a corresponding total preorder

annotations.note: Pulling back a strict weak order along a function `f` (`f ⁻¹'o r`) is a strict weak order via the `instIsStrictWeakOrder` instance, e.g. `f x < f y` for `f` into ℝ.

annotations.label: Partial order

anchor.snippet: A binary relation that is antisymmetric, transitive, and reflexive (but not necessarily total) is a partial order

annotations.note: `PartialOrder` extends `Preorder` (reflexive, transitive) with antisymmetry, exactly a reflexive antisymmetric transitive relation.

annotations.label: Totally ordered group

anchor.snippet: A group with a compatible total order is a totally ordered group

annotations.note: A totally ordered group is spelled `[Group α] [LinearOrder α] [IsOrderedMonoid α]`, i.e. a group with an order-compatible total order.
